title: Exhaled air purification unit and system for indoor multi-person venues or environments
abstract: Disclosed is an air purification system which includes an exhaled air collector that can be integral with a seat, attached to a seat, or located free standing, separated, and behind a seat. Further, the system includes an air purification chamber combined with the exhaled air collector. The air purification chamber can be remote from but connected to the exhaled air collector, such as connected by a conduit, or directly adjacent and attached thereto. The conduit can be remote from the chair, located within the chair, attached to the chair, or separated from the chair. The conduit can be comprised of plastic, metal, polymer, rubber, glass, or ceramics. The conduit can be an air suction conduit. The conduit can connect one or multiple exhaled air collectors to one or more remote air purification chambers, thereby creating a networked and connected air purification system.
claims:
Claims 1. An air handling device comprising an exhaled air collector and an air suction conduit, wherein the exhaled air collector is connected to the air suction conduit, wherein the air handling device is attached to, incorporated into, connected to, integrated with, part of, and/or separated and located behind a sitting apparatus, wherein the exhaled air collector collects, captures, and/or redirects exhaled air, wherein the air suction conduit transports the exhaled air in a downwards direction towards a floor or a ground, and wherein the air suction conduit comprises an opening that releases the exhaled air above the floor or the ground. 2. The air handling device of claim 1, wherein the air handling device does not filter and/or purify the exhaled air. 3. The air handling device of claim 2, wherein after the non-filtered and/or non- purified exhaled air is released from the air suction conduit into a location where the air handling device is located, the non-filtered and/or non-purified exhaled air is moved out of the location where the air handling device is located by air flow or suction. 4. The air handling device of claim 1, wherein the exhaled air collector houses, supports, or is attached to a display screen. 5. The air handling device of claim 1, further comprising an air suction intake located between the exhaled air collector and the air suction conduit, wherein the air suction conduit and/or the air suction intake comprise one or more fans. 6. The air handling device of claim 1, wherein a portion of the exhaled air collector is uncovered. 7. The air handling device of claim 1, wherein a portion of the exhaled air collector is covered by a grid, a grate, or a screen. 8. The air handling device of claim 1, wherein the exhaled air collector comprises or connects to an exhaled air analyzing device or component, and wherein the exhaled air analyzing device or component is capable of identifying a possible health pathology, illness, condition, or abnormality of an individual whose exhaled air was analyzed or screened by the exhaled air analyzing device or component. 9. The air handling device of claim 1, wherein the exhaled air collector comprises an exhaled air catch basin, and wherein the exhaled air catch basin further comprises an air suction intake located at the bottom of the exhaled air catch basin, on the side of the exhaled air catch basin, or between the air catch basin and the air suction conduit. 10. The air handling device of claim 1, wherein the exhaled air collector is capable of collecting a minimum of 0.2 liters of exhaled air every 6 seconds. 11. The air handling device of claim 4, wherein all or a portion of the display screen comprises a recessed exhaled air blocking surface. 12. The air handling device of claim 1, wherein the air suction conduit is attached to a backside of the sitting apparatus or built into a backside of the sitting apparatus or separated and positioned behind a back of the sitting apparatus. 13. The air handling device claim 1, wherein the air handling device is used in an indoor multi-seated venue or environment. 14. An air handling device comprising an exhaled air collector attached to, incorporated into, connected to, integrated with, part of, and/or separated and located behind a sitting apparatus, wherein the exhaled air collector comprises or is connected to 

an air suction intake, wherein the air suction intake is connected directly to an air purification chamber or is connected to an air suction conduit that leads to a remote air purification chamber. 15. The air handling device of claim 14, wherein the exhaled air collector houses, supports, or is attached to a display screen. 16. The air handling device of claim 14, wherein the exhaled air collector comprises or connects to an exhaled air analyzing device or component, and wherein the exhaled air analyzing device or component can identify a possible health pathology, illness, condition, or abnormality of an individual whose exhaled air was analyzed or screened. 17. The air handling device of claim 15, wherein all or a portion of the video display screen is a recessed exhaled air blocking surface. 18. The air handling device of claim 14, wherein all or a portion of the air suction conduit is attached to or built into or behind a back of the sitting apparatus. 19. The air handling device of claim 14, wherein one or more of the air suction conduit, the exhaled air collector, an exhaled air catch basin, or the air purification chamber comprise one or more fans. 20. The air handling device chamber of claim 14, wherein the air purification chamber comprises one or more UV light, microbicidal agent, liquid, or material, heating element, filter, High Efficiency Particulate Air (HEP A) filter, or combinations thereof. 21. The air handling device of claim 14, wherein the exhaled air collector or the air purification chamber is capable of collecting a minimum of 0.2 liters of exhaled air every 6 seconds. 22. The air handling device of claim 14, wherein a portion of the back side of the sitting apparatus is shaped, formed, and/or designed as the exhaled air collector. 23. The air handling device of claim 14, wherein the air handling device is that of an air purification unit. 24. The air handling device of claim 14, wherein the air handling device is releasably attachable to a back of the sitting apparatus. 25. The air handling device of claim 14, wherein the exhaled air collector is connected by conduits to one or more other exhaled air collectors, and wherein the plurality of connected exhaled air collectors are connected to one or more air purification chambers to form an air purification system 26. The air handling device of claim 14, wherein the air handling device connects to one or more of a Heating, Ventilation, and Air Conditioning (HVAC) system, an air handling system, an air purification chamber, or an air humidifier. 27. The air handling device of claim 14, wherein a portion of the exhaled air collector is uncovered. 28. The air handling device of claim 14, wherein a portion of the exhaled air collector is covered by a grid, a grate, or a screen. 29. The air handling device of claim 14, wherein the air handling device is located within an indoor multi-seated venue or environment. 30. The air handling device of claim 14, wherein the air handling device and/or the air purification chamber comprise one or more of an infrared (IR) sensor, an ozone sensor, a photosensor, or a carbon dioxide (CO2) sensor.
